title: Q5® Site-Directed Mutagenesis Kit Quick Protocol (E0554)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9rkt0","text":"This is the quick protocol for the Q5® Site-Directed Mutagenesis Kit (E0554).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"2rrel","text":"Primers should be designed with 5´ ends annealing back-to-back. We recommend using the NEB online design software, NEBaseChanger™.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":115,"length":14}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"D2EFB9B6C9B24E799F06E066041DC3EF","url":"http://nebasechanger.neb.com/"}}}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"d7c0r","text":"Please refer to Safety Data Sheets (SDS) for health and environmental hazards. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Assemble the following reagents in a thin-walled PCR tube. A B C 1 25 μl RXN FINAL CONC. 2 Q5 Hot Start High-Fidelity 2X Master Mix 12.5 μl 1X 3 10 μM Forward Primer 1.25 μl 0.5 μM 4 10 μM Reverse Primer 1.25 μl 0.5 μM 5 Template DNA (1–25 ng/μl) 1 μl 1-25 ng 6 Nuclease-free water 9.0 μl

Transfer to a thermalcycler and perform the following cycling conditions: A B C 1 STEP TEMP TIME 2 Initial Denaturation 98°C 30 seconds 3 25 Cycles 98°C 10 seconds 4 50–72°C* 10–30 seconds 5 72°C 20–30 seconds/kb 6 Final Extension 72°C 2 minutes 7 Hold 4–10°C * For mutagenic primers, please use the Ta provided by the online NEB primer design software, NEBaseChanger™ .
Assemble the following reagents: A B C 1 VOLUME FINAL CONC. 2 PCR Product 1 μl 3 2X KLD Reaction Buffer 5 μl 1X 4 10X KLD Enzyme Mix 1 μl 1X 5 Nuclease-free Water 3 μl
Mix well by pipetting up and down.
Add 5 µL, KLD mix from previous step to 50 µL, chemically-competent cells .
Spread 40 µL – 100 µL onto appropriate selection plate.
creator: New England   Biolabs
